Title: AI permit-status assistant for residents
BusinessProblem: Residents and 311 agents cannot see where a building permit is stuck, generating thousands of status calls per month.
CurrentProcess: Manual lookups across two systems; 6–8 minutes per call with frequent escalations.
ChallengeSummary: Give residents and agents self-service permit status without adding staff.
ProposedSolution: RAG assistant grounded on the permit system plus a status API, surfaced in a resident web chat and an agent-assist panel.
Services: Azure OpenAI;Azure AI Search;Azure Functions;Power Apps
BusinessValue: Deflects routine status calls, gives residents faster answers, and frees reviewers for complex cases.
Risks: Status API access approval; PII handling in chat transcripts.
DataDependencies: Permit DB read access; address normalization service.
Compliance: PII review needed for transcripts; no CJIS data involved.
NextStep: Scope a 2-week pilot with DOB IT.
Lessons: A thin status API plus RAG beat a full system rebuild; agents valued the assist panel more than the public chat.

Title: Benefits eligibility pre-screener
BusinessProblem: Applicants spend hours on applications they are ineligible for, and caseworkers spend time rejecting incomplete submissions.
CurrentProcess: Paper/PDF intake; eligibility determined late by a caseworker after submission.
ChallengeSummary: Help applicants self-assess eligibility before applying.
ProposedSolution: Conversational pre-screener that asks plain-language questions and explains likely eligibility with required documents, human-in-the-loop for edge cases.
BusinessValue: Reduces ineligible applications and caseworker rework; improves applicant experience and trust.
Risks: Must avoid giving authoritative determinations; clear disclaimers required.
Compliance: Sensitive program data; equity and accessibility review required.
NextStep: Align with HRA modernization roadmap via account team.
Lessons: Framing as 'pre-screen, not decision' was essential for agency comfort.

Title: 311 multilingual citizen assistant
BusinessProblem: Non-English speakers struggle to navigate 311 services and wait on long calls for routine requests.
CurrentProcess: Phone agents with live interpreters; long hold times for common requests.
ChallengeSummary: Provide reliable multilingual self-service for common 311 requests.
ProposedSolution: Multilingual assistant that handles common service requests and routes complex cases to agents with a translated summary.
BusinessValue: Improves equitable access and deflects routine multilingual calls.
ImpactMetric: Top 5 languages covered for common requests
Risks: Translation quality for civic terminology; agent trust.
Lessons: Civic-term glossary noticeably improved translation quality.

Title: Pothole image triage from 311 photos
BusinessProblem: Pothole reports arrive with photos but are triaged manually, delaying severity-based dispatch.
CurrentProcess: Staff visually review photos and assign priority by hand.
ChallengeSummary: Auto-classify pothole severity from citizen photos to speed dispatch.
ProposedSolution: Vision model that scores severity and clusters nearby reports to batch repairs.
BusinessValue: Faster severity-based dispatch and batched repairs.
ImpactMetric: Potential faster response on high-severity reports
Risks: Photo quality variance; liability of automated severity calls.
NextStep: Gather a labeled image set to validate accuracy.
Lessons: Clustering nearby reports may deliver more value than the severity score alone.

Title: Inspection report auto-draft
BusinessProblem: Inspectors spend significant time writing up reports from field notes after each visit.
CurrentProcess: Handwritten/voice notes transcribed later into a formatted report.
ChallengeSummary: Turn structured field notes into a compliant draft report.
ProposedSolution: Copilot-assisted drafting that converts checklist + voice notes into a formatted report for inspector review.
BusinessValue: Recovers inspector time and improves report consistency.
Risks: Accuracy of auto-drafted findings; mandatory human sign-off.
Compliance: Reports are legal records — human approval mandatory.
NextStep: Pilot with a small inspector cohort for accuracy and trust.
Lessons: Inspectors wanted to edit, not just accept — review UX matters more than raw generation.

Title: FOIL / records request triage
BusinessProblem: Public records requests are routed and de-duplicated manually, creating backlogs.
CurrentProcess: Staff read each request, categorize, and route to the owning agency.
ChallengeSummary: Auto-classify and route records requests with duplicate detection.
ProposedSolution: Classifier that tags request type, suggests the owning office, and flags likely duplicates.
BusinessValue: Could reduce routing time and backlog if data access is granted.
Risks: Sensitive request content; legal review of any automation.
DataDependencies: Historical requests with outcomes (not yet available).
Compliance: Records law constraints; legal sign-off required.
NextStep: Park until historical data access and legal guidance are available.
Lessons: Compelling idea but blocked on data and legal — good incubation candidate later.

Title: Transit incident summarizer for the ops center
BusinessProblem: During disruptions the ops center is flooded with feeds and must brief leadership quickly.
CurrentProcess: Operators manually compile updates from multiple feeds into a status brief.
ChallengeSummary: Auto-summarize live incident feeds into a rolling brief.
ProposedSolution: Streaming summarizer that fuses incident feeds into a concise, continuously updated brief with severity flags.
BusinessValue: Faster, clearer leadership briefings during disruptions.
Risks: Real-time feed access; accuracy under high load.
NextStep: Identify a follow-up owner and validate incident data feed access.
Lessons: Strong demo energy but no owner stepped up — at risk of stalling without intervention.

Title: Health inspection scheduling optimizer
BusinessProblem: Inspection routes are planned manually, leading to inefficient travel and missed risk-based prioritization.
CurrentProcess: Schedulers build routes by hand using spreadsheets and local knowledge.
ChallengeSummary: Optimize inspection scheduling by risk and geography.
ProposedSolution: Optimization service that prioritizes by risk score and clusters visits geographically, with a planner UI.
BusinessValue: More risk-aligned inspections and reduced travel time.
ImpactMetric: Est. 15% more high-risk inspections per week
Risks: Risk-scoring fairness; data quality of facility records.
NextStep: Confirm scheduling data feed and run a 3-week pilot with DOH ops.
Lessons: Explainability of the risk score was the key to inspector buy-in.

Title: Unemployment claims document extraction
BusinessProblem: Claims processors manually key data from uploaded documents, creating backlogs and errors.
CurrentProcess: Staff read each uploaded document and type fields into the claims system.
ChallengeSummary: Auto-extract structured fields from claim documents to speed processing.
ProposedSolution: Document-intelligence pipeline that extracts and validates fields, routing low-confidence items to a reviewer.
Services: Azure AI Document Intelligence;Azure Functions;Power Automate
BusinessValue: Cuts manual keying, reduces errors, and shortens claim cycle time.
ImpactMetric: Est. 40% less manual data entry on claims
Risks: Extraction accuracy on poor scans; sensitive PII.
Compliance: PII-heavy; strict access controls and audit required.
NextStep: Productionize the pipeline; reuse the DOCINTEL accelerator.
Lessons: Reusing the document-extraction accelerator saved an estimated half-day of build time.

Title: Multilingual benefits notices generator
BusinessProblem: Translating benefit notices into required languages is slow and inconsistent.
CurrentProcess: Notices drafted in English, then sent out for manual translation.
ChallengeSummary: Generate consistent multilingual notices with human review.
ProposedSolution: Template-driven generator that produces multilingual notices from structured case data with reviewer approval.
BusinessValue: Faster, more consistent multilingual citizen communications.
Risks: Legal accuracy of translated notices; approval workflow.
Compliance: Legally binding notices require certified review.
NextStep: Confirm legal review workflow before any pilot.
Lessons: Legal review is the gating step, not the generation quality.

Title: DMV appointment demand forecaster
BusinessProblem: Appointment capacity is set with little forecasting, causing long waits at peak times.
CurrentProcess: Capacity planned from static historical averages.
ChallengeSummary: Forecast demand to set smarter appointment capacity.
ProposedSolution: Forecasting model on historical appointment and seasonality data feeding a capacity-planning dashboard.
ImpactMetric: Potential reduction in peak wait times
Risks: Data quality; change management for staffing.
NextStep: Validate forecast accuracy against a holdout period.
Lessons: Operational adoption depends on branch staffing flexibility, not just model accuracy.

Title: Fleet maintenance failure predictor
BusinessProblem: Vehicle fleet failures are reactive, causing unplanned downtime.
CurrentProcess: Maintenance on fixed schedules; failures handled reactively.
ChallengeSummary: Predict failures from telematics to enable proactive maintenance.
ProposedSolution: Predictive model on telematics and maintenance history flagging at-risk vehicles.
BusinessValue: Would reduce downtime, but required telematics data is not collected.
Risks: Required sensor/telematics data does not exist today.
NextStep: Park — revisit only if telematics data collection is funded.
Lessons: No data foundation means no model — surfaced a data-collection gap worth a separate conversation.
